UX Design · Branding · IA · Hackathon · 2025

Revitalizing Caltec Solutions —
Rebrand, Redesign, Rebuild

Helping a compact IT service provider attract small business clients through a new brand identity, website redesign, and content strategy.

Role

UX Designer & Researcher

Timeline

Under 10 days

Event

Hackathon 2025

Team

Team 33 — 4 members

Result

Client's favourite — 1 of 5 teams

“Out of 5 teams at the Hackathon 2025, Caltec Solutions chose our redesign as their favourite. Here's how we rebranded and restructured an entire company's digital presence in under 10 days.”

Overview

A 20-year-old website trying to attract modern business clients.

Caltec Solutions is a compact IT service provider transitioning from B2C to B2B operations. Their existing website was built 20 years ago — full of tech jargon, hidden navigation, repetitive content, and too many redirections. They needed a complete rebrand and website overhaul to attract small business clients aged 30–40.

The brief — Caltec needed a design framework highlighting robust brand identity, digital visibility, and adaptable growth tactics.

My role

UX Designer & Researcher — across research, IA, branding, and content.

I contributed across the full project with specific ownership over several key deliverables:

Stakeholder interview — researched customer demographics, marketing goals, and Caltec's core selling points

Customer journey map — mapped Karen Chen's full journey from awareness to consideration

Sitemap restructure — co-designed the new IA to fix the existing navigation problems

Brand identity — co-designed new logo direction, business cards, and LinkedIn cover

ChatGPT content guide — built independently so Caltec could maintain their content post-handoff

Website redesign — contributed to Home, Business, and Contact page designs

Research

We interviewed the client and mapped what we learned.

I participated in a structured stakeholder interview covering four areas — customer demographics, marketing approach, task management, and Caltec's selling points. We synthesised the findings into a client needs map across current situation, goals, and constraints.

4

current customers — extremely small base, B2B transition was critical for growth

20yrs

old website — outdated content, tech jargon, poor navigation structure

5

competing teams — Caltec Solutions chose our solution as their favourite

Client needs map — current situation, goals, and constraints across stakeholder and team perspectives

Project direction — client needs mapped to our three core solutions: modern design, up-to-date content, easy maintenance

Persona & journey map

Karen Chen, 32. IT novice, cost-conscious, needs someone she can trust.

I mapped Karen's journey from her first Google search to evaluating Caltec's website. The key insight — she isn't just buying IT support. She's buying confidence and trust. The existing site gave her neither.

Persona — Karen Chen, 32, Notary Public Supervisor. IT novice, seeks reliable, personalized, cost-effective IT service.

Customer journey map — Awareness to Consideration. Karen searches Google, finds Caltec, evaluates their credibility.

IA audit & sitemap

The old site had 5 critical problems. We fixed all of them.

Tech jargon

Written for IT professionals, not small business owners who need plain language

Hidden links

Key pages buried deep in navigation or not linked from anywhere obvious

Too many redirections

Repetitive content creating confusion and unnecessary drop-off

Current sitemap audit — identified problems including small link buttons, hidden links, repetitive content, and tech jargon

New sitemap — grouped similar content, straight-forward navigation, commonly understood tech terms

Brand identity

Four core values. One new identity. Seven words.

We distilled Caltec's brand into four pillars: 20 years of experience, friendly and approachable service, long-term customized solutions, and one-stop IT support. The new slogan — “at your side · on your side” — captures all four in seven words.

Caltec's four core values — distilled from the stakeholder interview and used to anchor all brand decisions

New logo & slogan

Business card front

Business card back

LinkedIn cover — social asset designed to reinforce the new brand identity

Website redesign

Modern, trustworthy, and easy to maintain.

Full redesign of the Home, Business, and Contact pages. Clean layout, plain language, clear service descriptions, and a contact flow that doesn't require a phone call to get started.

Home page

First impression that builds trust

Hero with clear value prop, services overview, and a strong CTA above the fold.

Business page

Clear services for B2B clients

Grouped service offerings in plain language targeting small business owners aged 30–40.

Full website mockup — complete redesign across all pages

Design decisions

Why I designed it the way I did.

Visual language & style

Colour palette

Navy#042C53
Blue#185FA5
Mid blue#378ADD
Light#E6F1FB
Red accent#E24B4A

Blue signals trust and professionalism for an IT brand. Red accent retained from Caltec's existing logo for brand continuity — no unnecessary changes.

Typography

Caltec Solutions

Fraunces — brand identity, headers

Business IT Support

Space Grotesk — body copy, labels, UI

Why plain language instead of technical terminology?

Karen is not a tech person. Every technical term we removed was a barrier between her and trusting Caltec. Plain language isn't dumbing it down — it's respecting the user's time and meeting them where they are.

Why build a ChatGPT content guide?

Caltec had no in-house designer or copywriter. Without a guide, the website would go stale within months. The guide ensured our design decisions survived the handoff and gave Caltec ownership of their own content.

Why restructure the sitemap before designing any pages?

A beautiful design built on broken information architecture still fails users. We fixed the structure first so every page we designed had a clear purpose, the right content, and no unnecessary redirections.

Outcomes

Caltec chose our solution. Out of 5 teams.

Caltec Solutions chose Team 33 as their favourite out of 5 competing teams at the Hackathon 2025

Delivered a complete rebrand — new logo, slogan “at your side · on your side”, business cards, LinkedIn cover, and favicon

Redesigned 3 full website pages with a restructured sitemap and plain language content across all sections

Built a ChatGPT content guide independently — so Caltec could maintain their own content long after the hackathon ended

What I learned

Hackathons teach you to make decisions fast and commit to them. The ChatGPT guide came from a simple question I asked during the interview — “who's going to maintain this after we hand it off?” Nobody had an answer. So I built the answer. That instinct to think beyond the brief is something I carry into every project.
